deleteVmOnly.title: Delete VM of Subscription
deleteVmOnly.description: The subscription will remain active but the virtual machine will be deleted. You will need to configure a new VM to continue using the service.

deleteSubscription.title: Confirm Delete Subscription
deleteSubscription.description: This action will PERMANENTLY DELETE the subscription and the virtual machine (VM) on Oracle Cloud. All related data will be deleted and CANNOT BE RECOVERED. Please backup important data before deleting.

renewAndStart.title: ⚠️ Subscription Expired — Renewal Required
renewAndStart.description: This subscription's end date has passed. To start the VM, you need to renew for 1 more month.
renewAndStart.autoRenewHint: If enabled, the subscription will renew automatically each month. If disabled, the VM will be stopped when it expires again.
renewAndStart.insufficientFundsWarning: Please ensure you have sufficient balance before confirming.

vmNotConfigured.message1: VM not configured yet. Configure your VM to see server details.
vmNotConfigured.message2: No VM configured. Please configure your VM first.
vmNotConfigured.message4: Charts available when VM is running
vmNotConfigured.label: VM not configured yet

sshKeyRequest.title: Confirm New SSH Key Generation
sshKeyRequest.warningItem1: A new SSH key will be created and added to your VM
sshKeyRequest.warningItem2: Old key continues to work normally — you can keep using it
sshKeyRequest.warningItem3: Maximum 2 user SSH keys + 1 admin key (3 keys total)
sshKeyRequest.warningItem4: If you already have 2 user keys, the oldest user key will be automatically removed
sshKeyRequest.privateKeyOnScreen: Private key will be displayed directly on screen for you to download
sshKeyRequest.processStep1: System generates a new RSA 4096-bit SSH key pair
sshKeyRequest.processStep2: Public key is added to VM metadata
sshKeyRequest.processStep3: Private key is displayed directly on screen for you to download (one time only)
sshKeyRequest.processStep4: Old key continues to work, automatically removed when limit is exceeded

newSshKeyCreated.title: New SSH Key Created Successfully!
newSshKeyCreated.onceWarningTitle: ⚠️ This information is displayed only ONCE
newSshKeyCreated.onceWarningDesc: Please save it now. After closing this window, you will not be able to view the private key again.

resetPassword.title: Reset Windows Password
resetPassword.description: This will generate a new password for your Windows VM. The current password will be replaced. Make sure to save the new password.
resetPassword.resettingDesc: Connecting to your Windows VM and changing the password. Please wait...
resetPassword.successDesc: Your Windows VM password has been changed. Please save the new password below.
resetPassword.saveWarning: Please save this password now. You can view it later in the Windows RDP Credentials section.
resetPassword.newPasswordHint: 14-127 characters · must include: uppercase (A-Z), lowercase (a-z), digits (0-9), and special characters

validation.minLength: Password must be at least 14 characters long
validation.noUsername: Password must not contain the username "opc"
validation.needUpper: Password must include at least one uppercase letter (A-Z)
validation.needLower: Password must include at least one lowercase letter (a-z)
validation.needDigit: Password must include at least one digit (0-9)
validation.needSpecial: Password must include at least one special character (e.g. !@#$%)

actionOtp.sendError: Failed to send verification code. Please try again.

cloudConfig.title: Configure VM Instance
cloudConfig.vmNameNote: VM name will be auto-generated based on your email address in format:
cloudConfig.emailLabel: Email Address * (SSH keys will be sent here)
cloudConfig.vmShapeNote: Select VM shape first - OS options will be filtered by compatibility
cloudConfig.armCapacityNote: A1.Flex (ARM) instances often have limited capacity in Singapore region. If provisioning fails due to capacity, please try again later or switch to x86 shapes (E2.1.Micro, E3.Flex) which have better availability.
cloudConfig.armImageNote: Note: ARM images (🔷) only work with A1.Flex. x86 images (🔵) work with E2, E3, E4, Standard shapes.
